summary: xAI open-sourced Grok Build amid a data privacy controversy; Anthropic's Claude Code faces user pushback on instruction refusal and broken promises; OpenAI released Codex Micro hardware and GPT-5.6 in Microsoft 365 Copilot; user workflows highlight agent memory management, multi-model orchestration, and sandbox infrastructure. | Screening of official-vendor sources reveals strong mainstream product releases (Claude Code inside story, Mistral Vibe, Vercel Slack agent, Supabase OpenCode, Google ADK 2.0) and infra primitives (Cloudflare Precursor, Daytona Sandbox Firewall, Anthropic containment). User workflow signals from Amp and Fly.io blogs. Research on agent control primitives. Missing social/discussion field reports. | Major agent platform releases from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, GitHub, and others; user workflow improvements in Copilot code review and PR comments; infrastructure signals include browser-use, E2B, Vercel AI, and new sandbox/eval tooling. | Package sources show strong activity in agent observability/memory, MCP tooling, and coding agent detection. OpenAI Codex CLI and GitHub Copilot CLI release new versions. Agent airlock adds security layer for tool calls. Several new persistent memory and eval harness packages.

candidates.title: xAI open-sources Grok Build AI coding agent
candidates.why_it_matters: Major vendor enters open-source agent space; privacy concerns over repo uploads.

candidates.title: OpenAI releases Codex Micro macropad and Codex Micro

candidates.why_it_matters: Raises transparency and security concerns for agent ops.
